Off Calendar Sep-11-2025 Continued To Nov-05-25 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
Off Calendar Sep-15-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS ASSURANT, INC., AMERICAN BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY OF FLORIDA'S MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE.

The unopposed motion of Assurant, Inc. for admission pro hac vice in this matter is GRANTED.

A proposed order is submitted prior to the hearing; proposed order is rejected. Moving party to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the court's ruling and includes the name of counsel being admitted pro hac vice. Hard courtesy copy to be dropped in Dept. 302's inbox.

Judge: John M. True, Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/JMT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings
Off Calendar Sep-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Mark Epstein, Esq., of THE EPSTEIN GROUP Attorneys & Counselors at Law, Tel:(415) 863-5718 for plaintiff.
JACK V. VALINOTI, Esq., of WOMBLE BOND DICKINSON (US) LLP., Tel: 415.433.190 for defendant Comcast OTR1, LLC (Erroneously sued as Comcast QTR1, LLC).

Hearing was held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Defendant's counsel.Contests the tentative ruling denying its motion to compel arbitration and the relief Plaintiff seeks is public injunctive relief as opposed to private injunctive relief. Defense counsel noted that the Plaintiff's prayer for relief in the Complaint, specifically for restitution and injunctive relief, is not applicable to their client, as Comcast only provides services and does not cover or contract for warranties related to the issues raised. The matter argued/discussed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT COMCAST OTR1, LLC.'s Motion To Compel Arbitration And Stay Proceedings is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)

MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION
Off Calendar Sep-05-2025 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances:
Mark Epstein, Esq., of THE EPSTEIN GROUP Attorneys & Counselors at Law, Tel:(415) 863-5718 for plaintiff.
Edward J. Valdespino, Esq. of AIKEN LAW PC., Tel: 713.579.4940 for defendants SUREWAY, INC.; and AMERICAN BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY OF FLORIDA. (via Zoom Conference)

Hearing was held. A timely Notice of Contest was received from Defendant's counsel.Contesting regarding relief being sought by Plaintiff is "public" injunctive relief as opposed to "private" injunctive relief. The matter argued/discussed. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT AMERICAN BANKERS INSURANCE COMPANY OF FLORIDA, SUREWAY, INC.'s MOTION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION is taken under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn; Clerk: M. Macadangdang; Not Reported. (302/JMQ)
